data.description:
## Why
The dependency half of job 1: refute "package X doesn't exist / is hallucinated" (slopsquatting is a real,
high-base-rate class). The load-bearing risk is FALSE positives — an internal/workspace package or an
import-vs-distribution-name mismatch must NEVER be called absent. So this lane is confirm-only with a strict
abstain gauntlet.

## What
- **Manifest + lockfile parsing** across purl ecosystems (npm/PyPI/crates/Go/Maven/RubyGems/NuGet) to enumerate
  declared deps; **registry existence** probes (deps.dev as a direct oracle where available; per-registry
  fallback) to disprove a hallucination claim.
- The **abstain gauntlet** (only emit `refuted`/`abstain`, never `absent`): per-ecosystem name normalization
  (PEP 503, crates `-`≡`_`, Go `!`-escaping, npm scopes); workspace/monorepo membership (Cargo/pnpm/Go
  `replace`/Maven reactor); stdlib/builtin; import-name-vs-distribution-name (`bs4`→`beautifulsoup4`); any
  transient 429/5xx/timeout → `abstain`. Unknown ecosystem → `abstain`, never `absent`.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/deps.py`; reuse `syft`/manifest parsers + registry HTTP (descriptive UA, throttle,
  prefer index endpoints). Build on S1's contract + harness. Existence only — no vuln/CVE scanning here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest/lockfile enumeration across the purl ecosystems; registry existence via deps.dev + per-registry fallback.
- [ ] Abstain gauntlet enforced: normalization + workspace + stdlib + import/dist-name + transient-error → abstain, never absent.
- [ ] Unknown/unsupported ecosystem → abstain(reason); coverage recorded.
- [ ] Fail-open tested: offline / rate-limited / unknown-ecosystem / internal package → abstain, never a false "absent".
data.title: T0 dependency/package-existence (manifest + purl registries + abstain gauntlet)
